methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 53.2x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Space42, a publicly listed Space & Aerospace company formed through the merger of Bayanat and Yahsat, represents a consolidated player in a fragmented sector. The comparable universe includes 5 public peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private competitors including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, Planet Labs, and Firefly Aerospace—a total of 13 datapoints across the space sector. The company has raised $695M+ in ECA financing prior to its public listing.

Standard peer-median and public-comp anchoring methodologies are not applicable here. The peer-median approach fails due to insufficient narrow-filter matches: while 23 same-sector peers exist, zero meet stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier criteria simultaneously, rendering sector-wide medians unreliable. Public-comp anchoring cannot be executed because target revenue is not on file—a common constraint for private companies transitioning to public markets. The public Space & Aerospace median price-to-sales multiple stands at 53.2x across the 6 listed tickers, but absent Space42's disclosed revenue, this anchor cannot be operationalized.

Without applicable triangulation methods, a point valuation cannot be derived from the provided data. Valuation work would require either Space42's disclosed revenue figures or financial guidance from its public filings. Key uncertainties include post-merger revenue run-rate, capital intensity of operations, and time horizon to profitability relative to the listed comp set.
